description: Reference numeric layer applying Patch C to the conclusive merged release. Preserves the governing constrained latent-state evolution, preserves canonical variables/operators, and constrains deferred numeric semantics so the canon becomes reproducibly runnable through declared reference families, accessible-state approximations, tiered operator partition, deterministic reference runtime, normalization discipline, and auditable numeric provenance.

design_rule.principle: Do not replace latent dynamics with observation. Preserve canonical variables/operators; restore the constrained canonical update as the governing layer; add only adjunct states, derived variables, observation structure, and formal analyzability hooks.

canonical_core.description: Extension adding four-operator system (Enable, Express, Regulate, Constrain) mapped to canonical variables.

Enable.description: Expands viable state space, reduces dissipation baseline

Express.description: Activates flow and resolves gradients into observable dynamics

Regulate.description: Applies corrective pressure to maintain stability

Constrain.description: Removes invalid states; terminal boundary condition

notes: Operators are transformations on state, not state variables.
The canonical governing layer is the constrained evolution x_{t+1} = Pi_K(F(x_t)).
Operator composition is an implementation-level factorization of F followed by Pi_K, not a replacement for the canonical law.

H.name: History / hysteresis residue
H.description: Path-memory state encoding prior load, entrenched valleys, and trajectory-dependent regulatory residue.
H.role: Makes future reachable states depend on trajectory, not current state alone.

theta.name: Phase state
theta.description: Temporal phase for rhythmic redistribution dynamics.

omega.name: Rhythmic cadence
omega.description: Local update rate / cadence controlling phase advance and rhythmic coupling.

S.name: Sampling schema
S.description: Observation cadence, granularity, and refresh policy for each measured channel.

W.name: Window state
W.description: Observation window defining retrospective horizon, smoothing, and aggregation span.

Tau_obs.name: Observation lag
Tau_obs.description: Delay between latent state change and visible trace update.

A_obs.name: Aliasing risk
A_obs.description: Risk that sampling cadence or aggregation hides true rhythmic/system dynamics.

N.name: Noise / contamination
N.description: Exogenous or measurement-induced distortion within observed traces.

derived_variables.Delta_c: Legacy collapse margin / distance to collapse boundary

C_x.name: Cross-plane coherence
C_x.description: Degree of cross-plane alignment sufficient to preserve function under load.

components.C_load: Whether transferred load remains supportable across planes
components.C_topo: Whether topology/graph structure still supports viable flow

A_s.name: Accessible state-space volume proxy
A_s.description: Scalar proxy for remaining reachable viable state-space under current state, history, and boundary conditions.

L_P.name: Projection loss
L_P.description: Information loss induced by projection of full state into reduced observed state.

F.name: Failure channel vector
F.description: Mechanism-level decomposition of failure pressure before terminal collapse.

Delta_c_star.name: Patched collapse margin
Delta_c_star.description: Extended collapse margin incorporating history, alignment, accessible-state geometry, and projection loss.

Z.name: Trace bundle
Z.description: Time-indexed multichannel observation traces produced from the latent extended state.

O_q.name: Observability quality
O_q.description: Quality of the measurement arrangement for detecting structure relevant to diagnosis.

R_diag.name: Diagnostic resolution
R_diag.description: Effective resolution at which distinct failure channels can be separated in observation.

B_scope.name: Scope bandwidth
B_scope.description: Upper bound on temporal and structural variation that the current observation setup can faithfully capture.

M_view.name: View manifold
M_view.description: Set of rendered diagnostic views derived from traces, including waveform, phase portrait, load-transfer map, and boundary-distance trend.

J_F.name: Local Jacobian of latent evolution
J_F.description: Jacobian of the latent update map near the current state, used for local linearization, mode growth/decay analysis, and stability approximation.

Lambda_F.name: Local eigenmode spectrum
Lambda_F.description: Eigenvalues/eigenmodes of the local Jacobian or linearized constrained flow, used to characterize amplification, damping, oscillation, and bifurcation proximity.

V_K.name: Constraint-consistent Lyapunov candidate family
V_K.description: Local or regional energy-like functions compatible with the feasible set K, used to test monotonic decrease/invariance within admissible dynamics.

Sigma_K.name: Feasible-region transition signature
Sigma_K.description: Signature of how trajectories approach, cross, slide along, or are rejected by viability boundaries in K.

state_sets.X_c: Canonical state vector {Omega_V, Pi, T, Gamma}
state_sets.X_tilde: Extended state vector {Omega_V, Pi, T, Gamma, H, theta, omega, C_x, A_s, L_P, F}
state_sets.X_obs: Observation state {S, W, Tau_obs, A_obs, N, O_q, R_diag, B_scope}
state_sets.X_scope: Full instrument state {X_tilde, S, W, Tau_obs, A_obs, N, O_q, R_diag, B_scope, Z, M_view}
state_sets.K: Feasible / admissible state-set (constraint lattice, viability region, boundary conditions)
state_sets.Y: Reduced observed state produced by a constraint-derived observation projection

system_equations.Y: Y = P_K(X_tilde), with P_K derived from coordinates/readouts admissible under K
system_equations.Delta_c_star: Delta_c_star = f_star(Omega_V, Pi, Phi, C_x, H, A_s, L_P, J_F, Lambda_F, Sigma_K)
system_equations.K: K = feasible/admissible region induced by constraint lattice, boundary conditions, and governance rules
system_equations.F_latent: Latent unconstrained or pre-projection evolution map on x
system_equations.J_F: J_F(x) = d(Pi_K o F)/dx |_(x) or local active-set/tangent approximation
system_equations.V_K: V_K(x_{t+1}) - V_K(x_t) <= 0 inside invariant regions of K
system_equations.Sigma_K: sigma_K(x_t) = regime(interior, grazing, sliding, saturating, crossing)
system_equations.Delta_c: Delta_c ≈ d(x, boundary(K)) under legacy coarse geometry

accessible_state_geometry.A: Accessible viable state-set given current state, history, and boundary conditions.
interpretation: Two systems may share identical current Delta_c but differ in future reachable viability because H and A_s differ.
A_s decreases when history narrows future options or when regulation overcompresses viable alternatives.

F_locopt.description: Local optimization improves a local metric while degrading global coherence.

F_overctl.description: Over-control stabilizes short-term state while reducing adaptive capacity and reachable future options.
F_overctl.trigger_signature: Pi up, short-term Delta_c_star up, Omega_V down, A_s down

F_dimred.description: Dimensional reduction drives decisions from an overcompressed projection of the system.
F_dimred.trigger_signature: L_P up while policy/control depends primarily on Y = P(X_tilde)

F_pathlock.description: Historical grooves and hysteresis narrow viable future transitions.

collapse_rule.compatibility: Legacy collapse logic remains a valid lower-resolution approximation.

diagnostic_views.waveform: Time-series of selected channels, useful for surge, decay, oscillation, and lag detection.
diagnostic_views.phase_portrait: Pairwise or triadic plots (e.g., Gamma vs Pi, C_x vs Delta_c_star) showing attractors, loops, and hysteresis.
diagnostic_views.boundary_trend: Delta_c_star and A_s over time; estimates approach to instability.
diagnostic_views.cross_plane_alignment: C_phase, C_sign, C_load, C_topo traces for coherence decomposition.
diagnostic_views.failure_separation: Attempts to distinguish F_locopt, F_overctl, F_dimred, F_pathlock from their trace signatures.

usage_rule.statement: The scope is a diagnostic instrument, not a truth oracle.
implications: Observed traces Z are projections of latent state, not the latent state itself.
Good diagnosis depends on probe fit, sampling cadence, and window choice.
High projection loss or aliasing can produce convincing but false narratives.
Formal diagnosis should, where possible, report both margin proxies (Delta_c, Delta_c_star) and local dynamical hooks (J_F, Lambda_F, Sigma_K).

interpretive_notes: Operators remain transformations on state, not state variables.
Path dependence is now explicit through H and A_s.
Coherence is no longer scalar-only; Delta_c_star depends on cross-plane alignment C_x.
Rhythmic redistribution is modeled through theta and omega.
Dimensional reduction is explicitly represented by projection operator P and projection loss L_P.
Failure mechanisms are separated into channels without removing the terminal collapse boundary.
v3.9.48 defined the core dynamic variables and four-operator update logic.
v3.9.49 added path dependence, cross-plane coherence, accessible-state geometry, projection loss, and failure-channel separation.
v3.9.50 adds the missing observational instrument layer: how one actually sees the system move.
Latent state and observed trace are now explicitly separated: X_tilde is not Z, and Z is not M_view.
The model can now diagnose not only system dynamics but also failures of observation such as lag, aliasing, and overfiltered views.
The oscilloscope metaphor is formalized by Probe, Filter, Render, and Calibrate.
The governing law is again explicit: x_{t+1} = Pi_K(F(x_t)).
Enable/Express/Regulate/Constrain factor the latent update but do not displace the canonical constrained evolution.
Observation projection is now derived from the same constraint structure K that governs feasible state evolution.
Delta_c and Delta_c_star are reattached to computable local dynamical hooks via Jacobian, eigenmode, Lyapunov-style, and boundary-regime reasoning.
v3.9.52a adds machine-oriented execution semantics without introducing new canonical mathematics.
v3.9.52b binds the execution schema to interpreter-facing validation, initialization, scenario control, stability estimation, and trace emission contracts.
compatibility_notes: All prior v3.9.48 analyses remain valid as coarse projections of the patched system.
Set H = 0, C_x = 1, A_s = constant, L_P = 0, and ignore theta/omega to recover the legacy approximation.
Set O_q=1, Tau_obs=0, A_obs=0, N=0, and use identity Render(Filter(.)) for an idealized observation regime.
All prior analyses remain valid as latent-state analyses; this patch adds observation discipline rather than changing core dynamics.
Set O_q=1, Tau_obs=0, A_obs=0, N=0, and use identity Probe/Filter/Render approximations for an ideal instrument regime.
Ignore X_obs, Z, and M_view to recover the latent-state-only reading of v3.9.49.
Ignoring J_F, Lambda_F, V_K, and Sigma_K recovers the more heuristic v3.9.50 diagnostic reading.
Treating P_K as identity over admissible coordinates recovers an idealized no-extra-observation-compression regime.
Ignoring stability_computation_binding, validation_rules, scenario_schema, and trace_output_schema recovers the pure execution-contract view of v3.9.52a.
Ignoring Patch C family declarations recovers v3.9.52 symbolic flexibility.
Using deterministic_reference mode plus declared families yields a reproducible baseline interpreter.
Patch C constrains numeric semantics without altering the canonical governing layer.

summary: v3.9.48 remains the canonical low-resolution dynamic core.
v3.9.49 remains the latent-state extension adding path dependence, coherence decomposition, accessible-state geometry, projection loss, and failure-channel separation.
v3.9.50 remains the observability layer separating latent state, trace, and rendered view.
v3.9.51 reasserts the constrained canonical law as governing, reconnects margins to formal dynamical analysis, and unifies observation projection with the constraint lattice.

v3.9.53 adds the numeric grounding layer: constrained history dynamics, reference callable families, computable accessible-state approximations, structural operator tier separation, normalization rules, and reference-runtime provenance.
application_notes.identity: Oscilloscope-for-systems / multi-plane observability and failure-diagnostics instrument
use: Observe latent dynamics through partial traces without conflating trace with state.
Diagnose instability approach, path-lock, over-control, local optimization, and dimensional reduction.
Tune probe arrangement, sampling cadence, windowing, and rendering for better diagnostic fidelity.
non_use: Not a replacement for domain knowledge.

interpretation: F is the unconstrained evolution/generative step on state.
Pi_K is projection into the feasible region K defined by hard constraints, boundary conditions, and admissibility rules.
All higher-order operators, extended states, and observation structures must reduce to parameterization of F, deformation of K, or observation of x.
dominance_rule: Observation does not govern state; it samples or projects latent state.
Diagnostic views are valid only insofar as they remain traceable to x_{t+1} = Pi_K(F(x_t)).

reduction_requirements.Enable: parameterization/deformation of F and recovery of feasible motion within K
reduction_requirements.Express: activation of flow terms within F
reduction_requirements.Regulate: feedback action modifying F and, when necessary, local admissible motion near K
reduction_requirements.Constrain: explicit realization of Pi_K or terminal enforcement at the boundary of K
reduction_requirements.Probe_Filter_Render_Calibrate: observation-side transforms only; never substitutes for F or Pi_K

formal_stability.purpose: Reconnect practical margins (Delta_c, Delta_c_star) to formal local dynamical analysis without discarding observability realism.

objects.linearization: J_F(x) = d(Pi_K o F)/dx evaluated locally, or tangent/active-set approximation near nonsmooth boundaries.
objects.eigenmodes: Lambda_F = eig(J_F) or constrained tangent-spectrum equivalent.
objects.lyapunov_hooks: Candidate V_K decrease / invariance tests inside K.
objects.boundary_regime: Sigma_K tracks whether the trajectory is interior, grazing, sliding, saturating, or crossing a feasibility boundary.

interpretation_rules: Delta_c remains a coarse collapse-margin proxy.
Delta_c_star remains the richer diagnostic margin.
Neither margin is merely descriptive: each should be estimated from state, boundary geometry, and local dynamics.
Where Pi_K is nonsmooth, use active-set, tangent-cone, or piecewise linear approximations rather than assuming global smoothness.

projection_unification.statement: Observation projection is not independent of constraint projection.
projection_unification.relationship: P_K is derived from Pi_K and the feasible coordinate/readout structure induced by K.
rules: P_K governs admissible observation of that state.
Observation cannot introduce a projection theory detached from the governing constraint lattice.
Where dashboards/policies compress state, that compression is modeled as an additional lossy map after P_K, not as a peer to Pi_K.
consequences: Constraint and observation now live in one projection hierarchy.
Projection loss L_P becomes interpretable relative to the same feasible structure that governs evolution.
Instrument views remain subordinate to state evolution rather than replacing it.

patch_metadata.purpose: Constrain deferred numeric semantics so the canon becomes reproducibly runnable without changing canonical variables, governing law, or operator identity.

dominance_rule: rendered view never mutates latent truth for the same step
reference_execution_order: Compute operatorized latent proposal via Enable -> Express -> Regulate
Generate trace bundle Z = Probe(P_K(X_tilde), S, W)
Apply Calibrate to observation settings for next step

Enable.semantic_rule: Modifies latent viability and recoverability terms only.

Express.semantic_rule: Activates flow and gradient terms within latent evolution.

Regulate.semantic_rule: Applies corrective latent feedback without replacing constraint projection.

Constrain.semantic_rule: Realizes Pi_K and may saturate, clip, reject, slide, or terminally collapse.

P.semantic_rule: Derives admissible reduced observation from the constraint-consistent latent state.

Probe.semantic_rule: Samples the observation projection according to cadence and window configuration.

Filter.semantic_rule: Suppresses contamination or isolates bands without redefining latent truth.

Render.semantic_rule: Transforms trace data into interpretable views only.

Calibrate.semantic_rule: Tunes the observation stack for future steps and never retroactively mutates same-step latent state.

loss_model.interpretation: Information lost after admissible observation reduction.

forbidden_interpretations: P_K must not govern latent state evolution.
View-space compression must not be treated as equivalent to Pi_K.
Dashboard reduction is downstream of P_K, not peer to it.

latent_only.description: Run latent state evolution without the observation stack.

instrumented.description: Run latent state and the full observation stack.

diagnostic.description: Prioritize trace/view emission and runtime diagnostics.

strict_projection.description: Emit errors if P_K is invoked before Pi_K or if observation-side operators mutate latent truth.

observer_only.description: Accept externally supplied latent state and derive Y, Z, and M_view without evolving latent dynamics.

deterministic_reference.description: Runs the canon with declared numeric families and no implicit stochasticity.

stochastic_reference.description: Allows noise or randomized perturbation, but only with declared seed and distribution provenance.

stability_computation_binding.purpose: Provide concrete interpreter-side contracts for estimating margin, local stability, boundary regime, and collapse proximity from a step state.

extended_margin_estimator.contract: f_star(Omega_V, Pi, Phi, C_x, H, A_s, L_P, J_F, Lambda_F, Sigma_K) -> float

jacobian_estimator.contract: jacobian(Pi_K o F_latent, X_tilde) or active-set/tangent approximation

boundary_regime_estimator.contract: classify(x_t, x_t_plus_1, K) -> {interior, grazing, sliding, saturating, crossing, terminal}

lyapunov_hook_evaluator.contract: optional callable family returning local energy-like decrease/invariance evidence

computation_order: estimate J_F locally at admissible state
notes: These are interpreter contracts, not claims that one universal estimator exists for every domain.
Nonsmooth Pi_K requires active-set, tangent-cone, or piecewise approximations rather than naive global smoothness.

validation_rules.purpose: Reject malformed states, invalid execution orders, and semantically inconsistent runs before or during interpretation.
preflight: required bind points must be present for selected runtime mode
all required canonical state keys must exist
runtime mode must be one of declared runtime modes
scenario and initial condition schemas must validate
reference runtime modes require explicit declaration of Psi_family, f_family, and f_star_family when corresponding outputs are emitted
A_s emission requires explicit A_s_family declaration
H present requires lambda_H declaration unless default scalar is explicitly accepted
Delta_c_star emission without monotonicity declaration is invalid in strict reference mode
implicit callable defaults are forbidden in deterministic_reference mode

domain_rules: Delta_c and Delta_c_star are numeric when emitted
no NaN or infinite values in canonical state unless explicitly allowed by scenario
container_rules: X_c must include only canonical variables
X_tilde must include X_c plus only declared adjunct/derived slots
Y must not contain coordinates forbidden by P_K

order_rules: Enable before Express before Regulate before Constrain for latent execution
Calibrate must not retroactively mutate same-step latent state
write_barriers: Enable/Express/Regulate must not write Y, Z, or M_view

rules: P_K output must be derivable from admissible latent state only
L_P must be emitted when P_K performs non-identity reduction
dashboard or policy compression after P_K must be tagged as downstream_view_reduction
each trace event must include step index and timestamp or step_time
latent snapshot and observed snapshot must be separable
runtime diagnostics must include active runtime mode and collapse status

numeric_preflight: reference runtime modes require explicit declaration of Psi_family, f_family, and f_star_family when corresponding outputs are emitted
A_s emission requires explicit A_s_family declaration
H present requires lambda_H declaration unless default scalar is explicitly accepted
Delta_c_star emission without monotonicity declaration is invalid in strict reference mode
implicit callable defaults are forbidden in deterministic_reference mode
numeric_state_validation: Sigma_K must be one of declared categories
If Delta_c_star <= 0 then collapse flag must be true unless scenario explicitly defers collapse enforcement

initial_condition_schema.purpose: Define a complete, machine-validated starting point for an interpreter run.

scenario_schema.purpose: Define how an interpreter executes one run or a family of runs.
execution_semantics: A scenario may define one trajectory or a sweep over trajectories.
Scenario overrides must not introduce undeclared state keys.
Termination on collapse occurs when Delta_c_star <= 0 unless overridden by a stricter terminal rule.

trace_output_schema.purpose: Standardize auditable interpreter output for replay, debugging, and downstream analysis.

summary: Patch A remains incorporated in full.
Patch B remains incorporated in full.
Patch C is now incorporated in full.
Numeric semantics are now constrained through declared reference families, accessible-state approximations, normalization rules, and deterministic reference runtime contracts.
This file is the single-file conclusive release for v3.9.53.
release_notes.recommended_role: Use this as the canonical machine-readable handoff for reproducible reference-interpreter implementation.

design_rule_extension.statement: Patch C does not alter canonical ontology. It constrains numeric interpretation of already-declared adjunct and derived quantities so independent implementations can converge on reproducible behavior.

numeric_semantics_layer.reference_mode_goal: Provide the simplest nontrivial, deterministic, auditable numeric family for all required bind points.

affine_bounded.form: Psi(X_c, u) = clip(W_x * X_c + W_u * u + b, psi_min, psi_max)

saturating_nonlinear.form: Psi(X_c, u) = psi_scale * tanh(W_x * X_c + W_u * u + b)

cadence_relaxation.form: omega_next = clip(omega + alpha_omega * s(X_c,H,u) - beta_omega * omega, 0, omega_max)

phase_locked.form: omega_next = clip(omega + k_lock * phase_error(theta, X_c), 0, omega_max)

A_s_contract.statement: A_s remains a proxy, not a claim of exact state-space volume. Patch C requires each run to specify the approximation family used.
A_s_contract.normalization: A_s_norm in [0,1] required in reference mode

ellipsoidal_local.description: Approximate reachable viable neighborhood by a local ellipsoid in admissible coordinates.
ellipsoidal_local.computation: A_s proportional to det(Q)^0.5 for feasible local metric Q after clipping to K

box_bounded.description: Approximate accessible region by admissible axis-aligned side lengths.
box_bounded.computation: A_s proportional to product of clipped side lengths

simplex_tangent.description: Approximate accessible region in local tangent cone by simplex volume.
simplex_tangent.computation: A_s proportional to simplex volume in admissible tangent basis

interpretation_constraints: A_s must decrease when viable alternatives are explicitly removed by constraint tightening, all else equal
A_s must not increase under stricter K unless declared by recovery/restructuring mechanism
A_s = 0 implies no locally reachable viable continuation under the declared approximation family

operator_tier_partition.statement: Patch C makes the latent/observation split structural rather than only textual.

weighted_geometric_mean.form: C_x = prod_i max(eps, C_i)^(w_i), with sum_i w_i = 1
weighted_geometric_mean.reason: penalizes weak-link collapse without forcing min-operator brittleness

affine_margin.form: Delta_c = a_Omega * Omega_V + a_Pi * Pi - a_Phi * Phi + b

separable_penalty_margin.form: Delta_c_star = w0 + w1*Omega_V + w2*Pi + w3*C_x + w4*A_s - w5*Phi - w6*L_P - w7*h_norm(H) - w8*lambda_growth - w9*sigma_penalty
notes: sigma_penalty is ordinal penalty derived from Sigma_K

normalization_and_units.purpose: Prevent silent scale mismatch across implementations.
requirements: Each scenario must declare whether state variables are raw, normalized, or mixed-with-map
Reference mode requires normalized surrogates for Omega_V, Pi, Phi, A_s, L_P when cross-run comparison is requested
If raw units are used, a normalization_map must be supplied

deterministic_reference.description: Runs the canon with declared numeric families and no implicit stochasticity.

stochastic_reference.description: Allows noise or randomized perturbation, but only with declared seed and distribution provenance.

numeric_preflight: reference runtime modes require explicit declaration of Psi_family, f_family, and f_star_family when corresponding outputs are emitted
A_s emission requires explicit A_s_family declaration
H present requires lambda_H declaration unless default scalar is explicitly accepted
Delta_c_star emission without monotonicity declaration is invalid in strict reference mode
implicit callable defaults are forbidden in deterministic_reference mode
numeric_state_validation: Sigma_K must be one of declared categories
If Delta_c_star <= 0 then collapse flag must be true unless scenario explicitly defers collapse enforcement

compatibility_notes_extension: Ignoring Patch C family declarations recovers v3.9.52 symbolic flexibility.
Using deterministic_reference mode plus declared families yields a reproducible baseline interpreter.
Patch C constrains numeric semantics without altering the canonical governing layer.

Enable.description: Expands viable state space, reduces dissipation baseline, and softens path-lock by improving recoverability.

Express.description: Activates flow and resolves gradients into observable rhythmic dynamics.

Regulate.description: Applies corrective pressure to maintain stability, but can shrink adaptive freedom if overused.

Constrain.description: Removes invalid states; terminal boundary condition.

P.description: Maps full system state into a reduced observed state, but only through coordinates/readouts admissible under the governing constraint lattice K. Observation projection is derived from, and subordinate to, Pi_K rather than independent of it.

Probe.description: Chooses what aspects of the latent system are sensed, at what cadence, and through what window.

Filter.description: Suppresses contamination or isolates bands, but can destroy signal if overapplied.

Render.description: Transforms traces into interpretable views without claiming the view is the full state.

Calibrate.description: Tunes the observation stack so measured traces better reflect latent system dynamics.

Regulate.description: Applies corrective pressure to maintain stability, but can shrink adaptive freedom if overused.

Constrain.description: Removes invalid states; terminal boundary condition.
